seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must contain exactly 150-200 words.
new_constraint: The email must contain at least 300 words.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must contain exactly 150-200 words. The email must contain at least 300 words.
response:
I'm sorry, but I cannot fulfill that request exactly as written because it contains contradictory instructions. You asked for an email that must contain **exactly 150-200 words** but also that the email must contain **at least 300 words**. It is mathematically impossible for a text to be both within a 150-200 word range and simultaneously be 300 words or longer.

To help you, I can write **one** of the following for you:

1. **An email that is between 150 and 200 words.** (This is the standard length for a concise, professional request.)
2. **A much longer, detailed email that exceeds 300 words.** (This would be very long for a simple extension request.)

**Please tell me which length you would prefer so I can write the perfect email for you!**

***

**If you want the standard, professional 150-200 word version (Recommended):**

Subject: Extension Request - [Your Name] - [Course Name] Project

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension for the [Project Name] project, which is currently due on [Date, i.e., two days from now].

I have been making consistent progress on the assignment, and I am confident in the quality of the work I have produced thus far. However, I have recently encountered [briefly and professionally state the reason—e.g., an unforeseen family emergency, a heavier-than-anticipated workload in other required coursework, or a technical issue] that has significantly impacted the dedicated time I had allocated for the final stages of this project.

I take my commitment to this course and your class very seriously, and I do not wish to submit work that does not meet the high standards I aim for, nor the standards you expect. An additional [State specific number of days, e.g., 48 or 72] hours would allow me the necessary time to thoroughly review, polish, and submit my best possible final version.

Would it be possible to submit the project by [Propose a specific new date, e.g., Friday, October 27th] instead? I understand that deadlines are important, and I apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause. Thank you very much for considering my situation.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID]

***

**If you *insist* on the 300+ word length (Very detailed and lengthy):**

*(Note: This will be unusually long for this type of request, but it meets the word count requirement.)*

Subject: Formal Request for Extension Regarding the [Project Name] Submission - [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well and that your week is progressing smoothly despite the heavy academic schedule we are currently navigating. I am writing to you today with a significant and somewhat difficult request regarding the upcoming deadline for the [Project Name] assignment in [Course Name]. As you know, the submission window is rapidly approaching, set for this coming [Day of the week], [Date]. I have been diligently working on this project since the initial guidelines were provided, investing considerable intellectual energy and time into understanding the complex requirements of [mention a specific component of the project].

Regrettably, I find myself in a position where I must appeal for a brief, but substantial, extension. The reason necessitating this appeal stems from a confluence of external pressures that have unexpectedly materialized over the past few days. Specifically, [Elaborate extensively on the reason here. For example, if it's health-related, describe the situation briefly and professionally. If it's academic overload, detail which other courses are causing the strain and why.] This unforeseen circumstance has demanded a significant portion of my focus and energy that I had strategically reserved for the final refinement and critical review phases of this particular project.

I want to assure you, Professor, that this request is not born out of negligence or procrastination. My commitment to mastering the material and achieving a high level of academic performance in your course remains my absolute highest priority. I have already completed approximately [X]% of the assignment, and the core research and drafting are substantially complete. What I currently require is the dedicated time needed for the crucial post-drafting stages: meticulous editing, ensuring full citation accuracy according to [Style Guide, e.g., APA/MLA], and integrating final critical commentary to elevate the work from merely 'complete' to truly 'excellent.'

To provide adequate time for this essential final polish, I would be incredibly grateful if you would consider granting an extension until [Propose a specific new date, giving yourself at least 3-4 days]. I understand that modifying established deadlines requires administrative effort on your part, and I sincerely apologize for imposing this request. I truly value the material covered in your lectures and the rigor of your assignment expectations, which is why I do not want to compromise the final output due to an unforeseen delay. Thank you so much for your time, understanding, and consideration of this sensitive matter.

Warmest regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID]<turn|>
